title: stachys palustris var. glabra
intro: Stachys palustris var. glabra, also known as glabrous marsh woundwort, is a plant species within the mint family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's leaves and flowers can be consumed to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, while also supporting digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of Stachys palustris var. glabra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and prevent illness.
